* Starting each request on a new thread allows for better utilization of CPU resources and can improve responsiveness, but may lead to higher memory usage and increased context switching overhead.
* Creating smaller batches and launching each on a separate thread can improve parallelism and reduce the likelihood of thread contention, but may require more memory and coordination between threads.
* Using a fixed number of threads may not be optimal if the workload varies, and may lead to underutilization or overutilization of resources.
* Batch processing can reduce the number of threads required, but may increase the time to complete all requests if the web service is not responsive.
* Thread contention can occur when multiple threads are trying to access the same resource, leading to performance degradation and increased latency.
* Using a thread pool with a fixed size can help manage resources more efficiently and avoid the overhead of creating and destroying threads frequently.
* The choice between thread-based parallelism and batch processing depends on the specific requirements of the application, including the nature of the web service and the database.
* It is important to monitor and adjust the number of threads based on the workload and system resources to achieve optimal performance.
